ddls:
CREATE TABLE "hvac_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each HVAC model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the HVAC model */,
  "tonnage" TEXT /* Tonnage capacity of the model */,
  "seer_rating_max" INTEGER /* Maximum SEER rating of the model */,
  "noise_levels" TEXT /* Noise levels produced by the model (in dBA) */,
  "btu_range" TEXT /* Range of BTUs provided by the model */,
  "compressor_type" TEXT /* Type of compressor used in the model */,
  "model_year" TEXT /* Year the model was introduced */,
  "product_line" TEXT /* Product line of the model */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the model */,
  "warranty_period" TEXT /* Warranty period for the model */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time of the last update to the model's specifications */,
  "is_discontinued" INTEGER /* Indicates if the model has been discontinued */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the HVAC model to which these metrics belong */,
  "efficiency_rating" REAL /* Efficiency rating based on energy consumption (EER) */,
  "operating_noise" REAL /* Measured operating noise levels during usage (in dBA) */,
  "customer_feedback" TEXT /* Feedback provided by customers regarding model performance */,
  "date_of_measurement" TEXT /* Date of the performance measurement */,
  "operating_temperature_range" TEXT /* Range of temperatures at which the model operates efficiently */,
  "airflow_rate" REAL /* Airflow rate of the model (in CFM) */,
  "energy_consumption" REAL /* Average energy consumption (in kWh) */,
  "service_history_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service history entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES hvac_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_service_history_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_history_id") REFERENCES service_history ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role (e.g., Admin, Sales, Service) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., Read, Write, Update) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_history" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the HVAC model */,
  "service_date" TEXT /* Date of the service */,
  "service_description" TEXT /* Description of the service performed */,
  "service_cost" REAL /* Cost of the service */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the service */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_history_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES technicians ("technician_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_history_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES hvac_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the HVAC model */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer providing feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date of the feedback */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Detailed text of the customer feedback */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the customer (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_feedback_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES hvac_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "technicians" (
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each technician */,
  "technician_name" TEXT /* Name of the technician */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the technician */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the technician */,
  "years_of_experience" INTEGER /* Years of experience of the technician */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Specialization of the technician (e.g., installation, repair, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("technician_id")
)
